name: JUURYUU NAGE - SUPUREKKUSU (TEN-TON STYLE THROWS SUPLEX)

description.value:
Type: Taijutsu (Strike)

Rank: 10

Chakra Cost: 5

Learn DC: 24 | Successes: 4

Perform: 14 | Perform DC: 28

Components: M, E, Mas

This technique can only be performed when the user is behind the target and he is grappling the target. The user makes an opposed grapple check, and lifts the target up to then slam it back down on its head and back if the check succeeds. The suplex deals damage as if the target had fallen 20 feet, plus the user's Strength modifier, weapon damage bonus from Strength ranks, and an additional 1 point per active Strength rank. The user suffers 1d6 damage after successfully performing this technique.

The target must make a Fortitude save to avoid being stunned for 1 round afterwards. The target will be prone after being hit by the suplex, but no longer grappled. The user may make a Tumble check (DC 20) to stand as a free action after performing this technique. If the check fails, the user is also prone.

If this technique was performed as a follow-up to Kage Buyou, the grapple attempt automatically succeeds and the damage increases to two times falling damage plus the user's Strength modifier, weapon damage bonus from Strength ranks, and an additional 1 point per active Strength rank. However, the user suffers one-quarter the total damage, rounded up, when the technique is performed this way.

Requires: Combat Throw (f)

Mastery: At fifth level of mastery, the user no longer takes damage when performing this technique normally, and only takes one-tenth the total damage, rounded up, when performing the technique in conjunction with Kage Buyou.

system.target: One same- size or smaller creature

target.value: One same- size or smaller creature
